description: Impulse Response. We plot the impulse response functions for both the state variables (Sˆt, wˆt, μˆt, and gˆt) and the model-implied quantities (pd̂t, Et(rˆt+1), and rˆt) following a one-standard-deviation shock in one of the state variables at time 0. The response variables are standardized. Year 1 in the figure represents one period after the initial shock.

Extrapolative Beliefs, Surveys, and Prices. Panel A compares our filtered time-series of return extrapolation (S, left) and cash-flow extrapolation (w, right) with survey expectations of future returns (SURVEYSRET) and expectations of future long-term earnings growth (SURVEYCF), respectively. Panel B repeats the comparison, but uses only the extrapolative component of survey expectations (SURVEYSRETX and SURVEYCFX). For return expectations, we obtain such a component following the procedure in Greenwood and Shleifer (2014). For cash-flow expectations, we rely on the metric of investors’ experiences of past growth with fading memory from Nagel and Xu (2022). Panel C overlays our two time series of investor extrapolative expectations with the price–dividend ratio of the market. More details are in Section 3.
